name: Peach Pie Filling
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------FOR 7 QUARTS FILLING----------------------------
6 quarts Sliced fresh peaches
7 cups Granulated sugar
2 cups Clear Jel (tm); plus...
3 tbsp Clear Jel (tm)
5 1/4 cups Cold water
1 tsp Cinnamon (optional)
1 tsp Almond extract (opt.)
1 3/4 cups Bottled lemon juice
Quality: Select ripe, but firm fresh peaches. Red Haven, Redskin, Sun High, and other varieties of similar quality are suitable.
Yield: 1 quart or 7 quarts.
Procedure:
1. Peel the peaches by submerging them in boiling water for approximately 30-60 seconds, then placing them in cold water for 20 seconds. Remove the skins and slice into 1/2-inch thick pieces.
2. Place the peach slices in water with 1/8 tsp of ascorbic acid crystals or six 500-milligram vitamin C tablets dissolved in 1 gallon of water to prevent browning. Process 6 cups at a time in boiling water for 1 minute, stirring constantly until the water returns to a boil.
3. Drain the peach slices but keep them heated in a covered bowl or pot.
4. In a large kettle, combine the cold water, sugar, Clear Jel (tm), cinnamon, and almond extract (if desired). Stir over medium-high heat until the mixture thickens and begins to bubble.
5. Add lemon juice and continue boiling for 1 minute more, stirring constantly.
6. Gently fold in the drained peach slices and heat the mixture for an additional 3 minutes.
7. Fill jars without delay, leaving 1 inch of headspace. Seal the lids and process immediately.
